mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as lower discount rates boost equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in >60% of instances despite declining FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC <0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the unemployment rate above 8.0% signals weak labor market conditions, which typically precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or t-test p-value >0.05 for return differences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation and low federal funds rates create monetary policy uncertainty, which tends to depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in >60% of cases with CPIAUCSL >2.0% and FEDFUNDS <1.0%, or interaction effect IC <0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts and GDP growth indicate robust demand conditions, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 60d returns in >60% of instances with HOUST >580k and GDPC1 growth >2.0%, or interaction IC <0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production signals stronger supply-side activity, which tends to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in >60% of instances with INDPRO increases, or Rank IC <0.03 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment gains indicate a tightening labor market, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in >60% of instances with PAYEMS growth >100k, or t-test p-value >0.05 for return differences.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect strong consumer demand, which tends to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in >60% of instances with RSAFS >340k and HOUST >580k, or interaction IC <0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in inflation expectations combined with stagnant monetary policy creates equity market uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 60d returns in >60% of cases with CPIAUCSL >2.5% and FEDFUNDS <0.5%, or interaction IC <0.05.
